content:
Introduction Here is a little thought I had the other day:
If we got rid of the number 4, what would happen?
Now, I don&rsquo;t mean that we switch to base 9 and use the symbols 0, 1, 2, 3, 5, 6, 7, 8, 9 for our numbers (though that would be a fun way to confuse people). My disdain for the number 4 runs deeper than this. I want to know what happens when we continue to do math as normal, but we remove all 4&rsquo;s from existence.
The clearest way of demonstrating what I mean is to look at …
summary: What if we got rid of the number 4?
title: F*** Fours - An Exploration

content:
1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6 &hellip; damn, that&rsquo;s all I&rsquo;ve got.
See ya next time.
summary: Counting as high as I can
title: Sample Project
